Connect your Bank Accounts

Connecting your bank account to Rillet automates your financial processes by syncing transactions directly from your bank. This connection eliminates manual entry and reduces the risk of errors. With this seamless integration, your cash reconciliation stays up-to-date, enabling you to maintain accurate financial records. Whether you're handling daily transactions or preparing for audits, connecting a bank account streamlines your workflow and ensures reliability.

How It Works

When bank accounts are connected, Rillet uses Plaid to sync transactions directly from your bank. Transactions will appear in the bank transactions section of the cash reconciliation page.

  • Automatic Syncing: Transactions sync automatically after connecting.
  • 2FA Authentication: Depending on your bank's requirements, you may need to periodically reconnect your account due to two-factor authentication.

To Connect a Bank Account:

There are two ways to access the Banks page:

  1. Launchpad: Access your bank accounts through the Tech Stack Monitoring section from the Launchpad.

  1. Organization Settings:
    1. Click your company logo in the lower-left corner.
    2. Go to Organization Settings > Banks.

  1. Click the Add bank connection button.

  1. Then, select the Continue button.

  1. Enter your phone number. You can also choose the "Continue as guest" option if you prefer not to register.

Note:

    • Ensure the country code (e.g. +52 for Mexico) is correct.
    • Type your phone number in the corresponding field.
  1. Select the Continue button.

  1. In the pop-up window, search for and select your bank from the list (e.g., Bank of America).

Note: If your bank does not appear in the list, use the search bar to locate it.

  1. Once you've selected your bank, click the Continue to login button.

  1. Enter your bank login credentials.

Note: Make sure you check the necessary boxes (such as "Account and routing number") when prompted.

After connecting your bank account, you will see a message “Bank account connected successfully”, confirming that the connection was completed correctly.

How do I determine if my existing bank connection is broken?

You can check the status of your bank connections in the Tech Stack Monitoring section of the Launchpad tab. If an account needs re-authentication, it will be flagged with a warning icon, as shown in the example below:

  1. To update, click the error, and you will be redirected to the Bank Accounts page.
  2. Click Update Bank Connection and re-authenticate the account.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Setting Up Fields

Creating a Fields in Rillet allows you to customize your organizational data management. This process helps you tailor your workflows according to specific categories, enhancing your accounting and reporting capabilities. When you create a new field, you can define its nature (like "Department" or "Expense Type") and set specific rules for how it will be used within your organization. This customization not only improves clarity but also streamlines data entry.

To Create a New Field:

  1. Click your company logo in the lower-left corner
  2. Go to Organization Settings > Fields.

  1. Click on the Add Field button in the upper right corner of the screen.
  2. Enter a name for the field in the Field Name box.

  1. In the Values tab, select Add Value to add specific values applicable to the new field.

Note: For example, if creating a "Department" field, you might include values like "Sales," "Marketing," or "Finance."

  1. In the Rules tab, toggle on Mandatory if you want to make that rule mandatory.

Note: You can only create Rules if you have added Values.

  1. Select a display option in Show as. You can select:
    • Standalone field: Sets fields as text boxes. It is recommended for fields that apply to all transactions. e.g. Department, Location, Segment.
    • Free tagging field (only available for non-mandatory rules): Allows you to complete fields with multi-selectable tags.Recommended for fields that do not apply to all transactions. e.g. Event Costs, Software Capitalization Costs.

  1. When you are done, select Save to complete the process.

Managing Fields in Rillet:

  • Editing and Deleting Fields: You can rename unused fields without affecting existing data. However, used fields cannot be deleted unless all instances of their use are removed.
  • Mandatory Fields: If a field is marked as mandatory, users must fill it out before proceeding. For example, when creating a contract, if the "Customer Segment" field is mandatory, it must be selected to continue.
  • Free Tagging: This is ideal for optional fields, such as tagging multiple events like "Conference ABC" or "Workshop XYZ" in expense reports.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Edit Your Username

When you first get started with Rillet, your account will be set up with your first and last name, as well as your email address. You can edit your name afterwards, but not your email. This step-by-step tutorial will guide you to change your username. If you need to change your email, please get in touch with Rillet's support.

To edit your username:

  1. Click on your organization's icon in the lower left corner.
  2. Click User Preferences.

  1. Click Edit in the upper right corner to be able to edit the fields.
  2. Enter the name in the First Name field, and the last name in Last Name.

  1. Select Save to keep the changes.

  1. Once you're done, you will receive a confirmation that your changes have been saved successfully.

 

 

 

 

Switch Date Format

Customizing the date format in your app enhances user comfort and accessibility. Personalize this setting to improve readability and align the app's aesthetics with your preferences, bringing a more pleasant and efficient user experience.

Switch Date Format

If you want to switch date format, follow the steps below:

  1. Click on your organization's icon in the lower left corner.
  2. Click User Preferences.

  1. Go to the Appearance tab.
  2. Select the date format you want. (You can choose either MM/DD/YYYY or DD/MM/YYYY).

After you make this change, it will be applied to your account.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Getting Started
  2. Getting Started

Switch Between Light or Dark Mode

Toggling between light and dark modes in your app enhances user comfort and accessibility. Personalize these settings to reduce eye strain, improve readability, and align the app's aesthetics with your preferences, bringing a more pleasant and efficient user experience.

If you want to switch between light and dark mode, follow the steps below:

  1. Click on your organization's icon in the lower left corner.
  2. Click User Preferences.

  1. Go to the Appearance tab.
  2. Select Light or Dark.

After that, the appearance of your platform will change according to your selection and will be saved.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Getting Started
  2. Getting Started

Setting Up a Bank Account

Connecting your bank accounts to Rillet streamlines your financial processes. It allows for seamless invoice payment processing and efficient revenue collection. This integration helps in the accurate tracking of financial transactions, saves time, ensures security, and improves cash flow visibility.

To set up a bank account:

  1. Click on your company's icon in the lower left corner
  2. Click Organization Settings.

  1. Click Banks from your navigation sidebar, on the left. This will open your list of connected bank accounts. If you haven’t added a bank yet, this list will be empty.

4. Click + Add Institution Connection in the upper right corner to add a new bank account to this list.

  1. Select the bank you want to connect.
  2. If your bank is not on the list, select Other.

  1. Enter your phone number to connect with Plaid and click Continue.
  2. If you don't use Plaid, select Continue as Guest.

  1. Search for your bank. Then select Continue to login to log in to your account on your bank’s website.
  2. Click Submit to finish setting up a bank account.

Once you’ve completed logging in on your bank’s website, you will be directed back to Rillet and will see your bank listed on the Banks screen.

 

 

 

 

  1. Getting Started
  2. Getting Started

Best Practices to Structure your Chart of Accounts

The foundation of any ERP is a structured system of categorizing transactions. This starts with a well-defined Chart of Accounts, but can also include additional classifications such as Department, Customer Segments, etc.

When implementing Rillet, we often see SaaS companies with a chart of accounts that tries to break out each GL Account by both category and department. For example:

With this approach, your COA quickly becomes 200-300 accounts long, and you're spending hours at month-end splitting out costs to your various GL accounts.

If this is your case, consider a more streamlined setup where Rillet adopts the Chart of Accounts as your typical categories and sets up a Department or Team field. Then, use the SaaS P&L to very quickly create an allocated summary of your P&L. We'll walk you through the steps here in this article.

What is the SaaS P&L?

Before we start with the SaaS P&L, let's quickly review the traditional Income Statement.

The "typical" income statement begins with Revenues and Cost of Goods Sold, followed by a breakdown of expenses by GL Account categories, such as Salaries, Software, Meals, Travel, etc., without considering other classifications, like Department.

In contrast, the SaaS P&L summarizes total expenses in the main functional areas of SaaS, regardless of the type of cost. The areas are:

  • Sales & Marketing: Includes sales, marketing, sponsorships, account management, SDR, etc.
  • Research & Development: Includes engineering, product, design, etc.
  • General & Administrative: includes finance, HR, operations, IT, and CEO costs.
  • Cost of Revenue: includes Customer Success and technology supporting the customers, such as hosting costs

Get Set Up in Rillet

To fully use the power of Rillet's automated classification system, follow these three steps:

  1. Create a Chart of Accounts which breaks down GL Accounts into their "types" such as Salaries, Payroll Taxes, Benefits, Software, Travel, etc.

  1. Create a Field for your Departments (Sales, Marketing, Engineering, Product, Finance, HR, etc.)
  2. Set up the mapping of your Departments in the SaaS P&L Settings.

Now you have a clean and simple categorization system that allows you to create an Income Statement by Department.

  1. Getting Started
  2. Getting Started

Changing Member Roles

Changing permissions allows you to update a member's role within your organization, granting or restricting access to certain features and functionalities based on their responsibilities.

To change a member's permissions:

  1. Click the company icon in the top right corner of the screen.
  2. Go to Organization Settings > Members & Roles.

  1. Find the member whose permissions you wish to change.
  2. Click the three dots (•••) next to the member's name.
  3. Select Edit Role from the dropdown menu.

  1. In the Update Member dialog box, choose the desired role from the Role dropdown.
    1. Admin
    2. Accountant

  1. Click Update Member to save the changes.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Getting Started
  2. Getting Started

Archiving a Member

Archiving a member removes their access to the organization's account without permanently deleting their information. This is useful for managing team members who no longer need access, while still retaining historical data.

To Archive a Member:

  1. Click the company icon in the top right corner of the screen.
  2. Go to Organization Settings > Members & Roles.

  1. Find the member you wish to archive in the list.
  2. Click the three dots (•••) next to the member's name.
  3. Select Archive Member. A confirmation dialog will appear. Review the message indicating that the member will no longer have access to Rillet.

  1. Click Archive Member to confirm the action.

Once a member is archived, the action is permanent.

 

 

  1. Getting Started
  2. Getting Started

Add an Account

Your chart of accounts is the backbone of your financial organization, helping you categorize every transaction. This guide will show you how to add new accounts and deactivate old ones you no longer need.

To add an account:

  1. Click your company's logo on the lower left corner.
  2. Select Organization Settings > Chart of Accounts.
  3. Click the + Add Account button, located in the top right corner.

  1. A window will pop up. Enter all the necessary information for your new account, such as:
    • Account Number
    • Name
    • Type
    • Subtype
    • Parent Grouping
  2. Once you've filled in the details, click Save.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Launchpad Overview

The Launchpad provides a quick overview of key metrics and offers easy access to essential data, making it an efficient tool for monitoring your business performance. You can customize it to fit your needs, ensuring you have all the important details at your fingertips.

Follow the steps below to get an overview of how the Launchpad works:

  1. Go to Launchpad in the main menu.
  2. In + Add Metric, you can select:
    • ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue): The total amount of predictable revenue your business expects to earn each year from subscriptions or contracts.
    • Outstanding AR (Accounts Receivable): The total amount of money your customers owe you that hasn’t been paid yet.
    • Outstanding AP (Accounts Payable): The total amount of money your business owes to suppliers and vendors that hasn't been paid yet.
    • Overdue AR (Overdue Accounts Receivable): The amount of accounts receivable that are past due must be collected.
    • Gross Burn: The total amount of money your business is spending, typically on operating expenses, without any revenue being considered.
    • Cash Balance: The amount of cash your business has available on hand, which is important for managing liquidity.
    • Runway: The amount of time your business can continue operating before it runs out of cash, based on your current cash balance and burn rate.
    • Net Burn: The amount of money your business loses each month after accounting for both revenue and expenses.
    • Net Revenue Retention: A measure of revenue growth or loss from existing customers, excluding new customer revenue, showing how well you're retaining business.

Note:

    • A total of 6 metrics can be selected.
    • You can reposition the metrics by holding and dragging them to your preferred location.
  1. (Optional): Click the minus (-) icon to delete any selected metric.

  1. In the Workflow snapshot, you can view:
    • Cash Transactions to be reconciled
    • Invoices to be sent
    • Contracts to be reviewed
    • Bills to be paid
    • Last closed month
  2. In Reports, select + Add report. Some available options are:
    • Income Statement
    • Balance Sheet
    • Cashflow Statement
    • SaaS P&L
    • AR Aging
    • Revenue by Customer
    • AP Aging

Note:

To view more details for each option in the Reports and Workflow snapshot, click on any of them, and you will be redirected to the corresponding page.

  1. In Tech stack monitoring, you can set up:
    • Banking: Select Set up Banking to add new bank connections or update existing ones.
    • Integrations: Select Browse Integrations for options related to:
      • Payroll
      • Payments & Tax
      • CRM
      • Accounts Payable

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Getting Started
  2. Aura AI Assistant

Use the AI Assistant to Analyze Your Financial Data

Speak to your financial data using natural language. You can ask questions like "What were my top 10 vendors in 2024?" or "Perform a flux analysis for February 2025" to get meaningful insights quickly.

To use the AI assistant:

  1. Go to the main menu and click the AI Assistant icon located on the left-hand side of the screen.

  1. Select from the suggested queries or enter your query in the prompt window (e.g., "What were my top 10 vendors in 2024?" or "Perform a flux analysis for February 2025").

Wait a few seconds while the AI processes your data. The result will be displayed, usually as a list or table depending on the type of question.

Tips:

  • To troubleshoot, try closing the AI window and reopening it before submitting a new question.
  • Be as specific as possible in your queries (include timeframes, departments, etc.).

To Try Out AI-Powered Queries:

Here are some examples of questions you can ask Rillet AI to get started:

  • What were my top 10 vendors in 2024?
  • What were the top expenses by vendor for the Marketing department last month?
  • Draft an ASC 606 revenue recognition technical memo for my SaaS revenue including SSP allocation assumptions.
  • Perform a flux analysis for February 2025.
  • What customers churned last month?
  • What accruals do I need to record for March?

You can simply type any of these questions into the prompt window and wait for the AI to generate a response.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Getting Started
  2. Aura AI Assistant

AuraAI Prompt Examples

AuraAI is has many great uses, but if you're struggling with where to start, here's a great list of prompts you can ask your Aura AI.

Documentation

  • Draft my fixed asset policy
  • Create an ASC 606 Revenue recognition policy for my usage based contracts with minimum commitments
  • What is the accounting treatment for for office leases under ASC 842

Expenses

  • What were my Top 10 Vendor last month
    • What were the top 10 vendors the month prior to that?
    • Can you create a comparison of the two
  • What were the largest marketing team expenses in Mar 2025
  • Find duplicate vendors
    • Merge the duplicate vendors
  • Why did my travel expense account significantly fluctuate in Jan 2025?

Revenue

  • Show me a line graph with my revenue by customer for 2024
    • Then: Compare to revenue from 2025
  • What were my top 10 customers last month
  • What were my top 10 customers last month based on revenue
  • What were my top customers last month based on ARR
  • Give me a list of all invoices from last month
  • What customers had the most amount of invoices by dollar amount last month
  • What were my top 5 customers based on ARR in Jan 2025
  • What customers churned last month (flagged as an issue)
  • What contributed to contraction last month
  • How much total expansion was there in July 2025
  • What invoices are pending payment? What customers haven’t paid yet (Ste
  • How many unpaid invoices do I currently have
  • How many unbilled invoices do I have with a date before today
  • Can you list all unpaid invoices
  • What customers are over 90 days overdue?
    • Then: Can you total that by customer

General Accounting

  • Perform a Flux analysis for Jan 2025
  • What accruals do I need this month
  • Create an income statement for Jan 2025
  • Create a suggested journal entry for XYZ
  • What is my Net Income per month for 2025
  • What is my gross margin by month for 2024

If you'd don't see as specific data set or prompt in the Aura AI, just let us know that you'd like it to be added!

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable

Getting Started with Accounts Receivable

As a Rillet user, you have the opportunity to manage your Invoicing and revenue recognition all within the Accounts Receivable module. From setting up customers to managing contracts and collecting payments, you can use Rillet to manage the entire contract to cash process

To access the Accounts Receivable management features, first go to your main navigation sidebar and click the contract icon.

You’ll see a list of navigation options appear when you do so. These options are customers, products, contracts, invoices and credit memos.

Let’s look at each of these sections and learn what they can help you achieve. You can switch between each one by clicking the tabs at the top of your screen.

Customers

The Customers section of your Accounts Receivable window is where you can keep track of all the customers your company delivers services to. You can add, edit or delete customers from this window. To learn more about how to perform these actions, read this article: 📄 Add a New Customer to Rillet

Products

The Products section is where you’ll record what products or services you’re delivering to your clients. For example, a subscription plan or an SaaS service could be products you record here. To learn more about adding products to this list, read this article: 📄 Create a New Product

Contracts

Contracts are one of the offerings unique to Rillet, and they’re designed to streamline your billing processes by providing a way to collect and automatically send multiple recurring invoices from one location.

Think of it this way: If you use another system, but need to bill a customer monthly for a year-long subscription, you’d need to create a new invoice from scratch every month for that monthly amount. With Rillet, you can simplify this process by creating one yearly contract with a set of 12 monthly invoices. Those invoices will then be sent out automatically to your customer every month, without any additional work on your end.

The Contracts section is where you can add and manage all your contracts. To learn more about adding and editing contracts, read this article: 📄 Add a New Customer to Rillet

Note

In order to set up a contract, you will need to first set up its corresponding customer and product in the previous two screens.

Invoices

The Invoices section is where you’ll be able to view a list of all your invoices, both billed and unbilled. You’ll see each invoice tagged to a corresponding customer, which you added in the previous Customers screen.

Credit Memos

If you’re unfamiliar with credit memos, think of them as a way to credit your customers with money that will then be deducted from the amount they owe you. For example, if they signed a yearly contract for $12,000, but you later decide to offer them a free month of service, then you will create a credit memo for $1,000 on this screen. Now, the invoice or contract record will show they only owe $11,000.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Contracts

Managing Contracts

Once a contract is created, you can edit, delete, amend, or review its financial impact to ensure accurate tracking and adjustments. Below are the steps for managing contracts in Rillet:

To Edit Contract:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the contract you want to edit.
  2. Select Edit Contract from the dropdown menu.

  1. Modify the necessary Contract Details.
  2. Click Confirm Contract to save your changes.

To Delete Contract:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the contract you want to delete.
  2. Select Delete Contract from the dropdown menu.

  1. Confirm the deletion by clicking Delete or Cancel if you don't wish to proceed.

To Amend Contract:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the contract you want to amend.
  2. Select Amend Contract from the dropdown menu.

  1. Click the Continue button.

  1. Enter the Amendment Reason.
  2. Update the necessary fields.

  1. Click Confirm Contract to finalize the changes.

To View GL Impact:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the contract you want to analyze.
  2. Select GL Impact from the dropdown menu.

  1. A report will appear, displaying the following columns:
    1. Account #: The account number associated with the transaction.
    2. Account: The account name corresponding to each transaction.
    3. Date: The date of the transaction.
    4. Transaction ID: An identifier for the transaction.
    5. Description: A brief description that typically includes the contract name and product.
    6. Debit: The amount debited from the account.
    7. Credit: The amount credited to the account.
  2. To download the report for further analysis, click Export in the upper-right corner of the GL Impact window.

The data will automatically download in XLSX format.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Contracts

Managing Contracts

Once a contract is created, you can edit, delete, amend, or review its financial impact to ensure accurate tracking and adjustments. Below are the steps for managing contracts in Rillet:

To Edit Contract:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the contract you want to edit.
  2. Select Edit Contract from the dropdown menu.

  1. Modify the necessary Contract Details.
  2. Click Confirm Contract to save your changes.

To Delete Contract:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the contract you want to delete.
  2. Select Delete Contract from the dropdown menu.

  1. Confirm the deletion by clicking Delete or Cancel if you don't wish to proceed.

To Amend Contract:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the contract you want to amend.
  2. Select Amend Contract from the dropdown menu.

  1. Click the Continue button.

  1. Enter the Amendment Reason.
  2. Update the necessary fields.

  1. Click Confirm Contract to finalize the changes.

To View GL Impact:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the contract you want to analyze.
  2. Select GL Impact from the dropdown menu.

  1. A report will appear, displaying the following columns:
    1. Account #: The account number associated with the transaction.
    2. Account: The account name corresponding to each transaction.
    3. Date: The date of the transaction.
    4. Transaction ID: An identifier for the transaction.
    5. Description: A brief description that typically includes the contract name and product.
    6. Debit: The amount debited from the account.
    7. Credit: The amount credited to the account.
  2. To download the report for further analysis, click Export in the upper-right corner of the GL Impact window.

The data will automatically download in XLSX format.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Contracts

How to Create an Auto-Renewing Contract

An auto-renewing contract, also known as an open-ended or evergreen contract, is designed to automatically generate invoices at specified intervals without a defined end date. This allows for continuous revenue collection and maintains a seamless billing process for your customers.

Note:

Before creating an auto-renewing contract, ensure that you have already created your client and associated products. For detailed instructions, refer to the Add a Contract article.

To Create an Auto-renewing Contract:

  1. Go to the client for whom you want to create the contract.

  1. Click the Contracts tab and then select + Add Contract.

  1. Enter Contract Details:
    • Contract Name: Enter a descriptive name (e.g., "Auto-Renewal Contract").
    • Contract Type: Select New Sales or another relevant type.
    • Start Date: Set a start date for the contract (e.g., January 1, 2025).
    • Duration: Select Open-ended to allow for automatic renewals without a specified end date.
  2. Click Next Step →.

  1. Click + Add Product.

  1. Choose a Fixed Recurring Product from your list (e.g., "Demo Product").

Note:

Ensure that the product is fixed and recurring. One-time products will not automatically renew.

  1. Set Product Details:
    • Pricing: Input the price per month (e.g., $100) and specify the quantity (e.g., 2 users).
    • Start Date: This will typically default to the contract start date.
    • Tax Settings: If applicable, you can toggle taxes on or off, depending on your agreement.
  2. Click Next Step to proceed to the Invoicing section.

  1. Choose how often you'd like invoices to be generated (e.g., Quarterly).
  2. Click Next Step →.

  1. Once more, click Next Step →.

  1. Click Confirm Contract to finalize.

Automatic Invoice Generation

Once the contract is established, the system will automatically generate the first set of invoices corresponding to the defined schedule. For instance, if you have set quarterly invoicing, you can expect invoices to appear periodically in the system, maintaining up to 12 months of future invoices in the queue.

To End Contract:

  1. Click the three dots (...) next to the contract and select End Contract.

  1. Specify the desired end date and confirm. This process will remove future invoices beyond the selected date.

Additional Notes

  • One-Time Fees: You can add one-time fees (e.g., for implementation or setup) to the contract. It's important to note that these fees will not recur, meaning they will not generate future invoices. This feature is beneficial for upfront costs or activation fees.
  • Tax Settings: It may be advisable to turn off the tax option in your instance if taxes are not applicable. This will simplify the invoicing process, as you won’t need to select this option each time you create a contract.
  • Recurring Fees: Only the fixed recurring products will continue to generate invoices. Ensure that the products selected for the auto-renewing contract are set as fixed and recurring to maintain consistent billing.


 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Contracts

Viewing GL Impact: Understanding the Financial Implications of Usage Cycles

After setting up your contracts, you can review the General Ledger (GL) impact of these contracts.

To View GL Impact:

  1. Click the three-dot (...) menu next to the contract name.
  2. Select GL impact from the dropdown menu.

Differences in GL Impact between Usage Cycle Monthly and Usage Cycle Contract

GL Impact: Usage Cycle Monthly

The accounting for a monthly usage cycle displays revenues on a month-by-month basis and reflects the associated monthly expenses. Any usage that exceeds the monthly minimum is recorded as additional revenue.

GL Impact: Usage Cycle Contract

For a usage cycle based on the contract, revenues are recognized either all at once if consumed at the beginning or distributed based on resource utilization. This may result in variations between accounting periods.

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Create a Refund Transaction

A refund transaction is created to return funds to a customer based on a previously issued credit memo. This process typically involves generating a standalone credit memo, paying the refund, and reconciling the payment with the credit memo. Once reconciled, the credit memo's status changes to "Refunded".

Create a Credit Memo

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Credit Memos.

  1. Select Add Credit Memo.

  1. Select a Customer.

Do not select an invoice. This ensures the credit memo is standalone and not tied to a specific invoice.

  1. Select the Credit Memo date.

  1. Select Product/s and input the additional information such as revenue dates.
  2. Click Save to finalize the credit memo.

The credit memo status will now be Unpaid.

Process & Match the Refund Payment

  1. Once the payment has been made, go to Cash Reconciliation.
  2. Locate the refund payment and match it to the credit memo.
  3. Click Match.

  1. Once matched, the credit memo status will change from Unpaid to Refunded.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Auto-Pay Settings

Auto-Pay settings allows you to automate recurring customer payments, which streamlines the invoice management process and reduces the need for manual intervention.

Determine Auto-Pay Date

To determine the Auto-Pay date:

  1. Go to Organization Settings > Invoices > Communications.
  2. Set the Payment Timing.
    1. Sent date
    2. Invoice date
    3. Invoice due date
  3. Click the Save button to apply your changes.

Enable Auto-Pay on a Customer:

To enable Auto-Pay on a customer:

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Customers.

  1. Select the customer for whom you want to enable automatic payments.

  1. In the Overview section, locate the Automatic payments toggle and switch it ON.

  1. Click the Save Changes button.

Once enabled, invoices for this customer will be charged automatically without manual intervention.

What Happens When You Send an Auto-Pay Invoice:

  • If the payment was successfully processed: The invoice will show as paid, with an amount due of $0.
  • If the payment failed: The invoice will show an amount due that still needs to be paid.
  • Payment Timing Considerations: If the invoice is sent before the payment is processed, the amount due may be shown even if the payment is successfully processed later. The invoice date and the sent date will always be the same for auto-pay invoices.

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Apply an Open Credit to an Invoice

Applying an open credit to an invoice allows you to reduce the balance of the invoice using available credit. The credit must be lower than the total invoice amount for the process to be valid. This is a common procedure for handling customer credits that can be used to offset outstanding invoices.

To apply an open credit to an invoice:

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Credit Memos.

  1. Locate the open credit you want to apply.
  2. Use the three dots icon (more options) and select Apply to an Invoice.

  1. Select the invoice you wish to apply the credit to.

Ensure that the amount of the open credit is lower than the total amount of the invoice.

  1. Click Apply to confirm the credit application for the selected invoice.

Additional Notes:

  • The open credit can only be applied if the credit amount is less than the invoice total. If the credit amount exceeds the invoice total, it cannot be applied until adjustments are made.
  • Always double-check the remaining balance on the invoice after applying the credit to ensure accurate accounting records.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Customer Overpayment

Customer overpayments occur when a customer pays more than the amount due on an invoice. This can happen due to various reasons, such as:

  • The customer mistakenly enters a higher payment amount.
  • Duplicate payments are made.
  • Rounding differences in currency conversion.
  • The customer intends to prepay for future services.

Since you have received more money than billed, the excess amount is recorded as a liability until it is refunded or applied to a future invoice.

In this article we will walkthrough the reccomended steps for recording overpayments in Rillet

Setting Up Accounts & Products

First, we will set up the proper objects in Rillet to ensure proper recording.

Create a Customer Overpayment Account

In your Chart of Accounts, create a Liability account for your customer overpayments

For example:

Create an Overpayments Product

In your Product listing, create a product for Overpayment Credit using a Non-Revenue product. The Account used should be the same account that was created above.

Now you're all set to record an overpayment!

Recording an Overpayment

When matching a payment where the bank transaction amount is > the invoice total, you will see the option for Match & Adjust:

Click Match & Adjust and then user the Customer Overpayments Account for the adjustment.

This will create a liability for the amount of the overpayment.

Optional: Creating a Customer Credit

Navigate to the customer's profile and create a new credit memo using the Overpayments Credit product. The revenue recognition dates can be the same as the Credit Memo Date.

This will create an unpaid credit on the Customer's profile

That this step is option if you are refunding the credit memo directly to the customer.

Refunding an Overpayment

When you refund the customer you have two options:

  • Record the refund as a Quick Entry to Customer Overpayments (if no credit has been created).
  • Match the refund to the Customer's Credit Memo in the Cash Reconciliation

Applying an Overpayment to an Invoice

When the customer has a future invoice created, you can then apply the Overpayment Credit Memo to the future invoice

This application will reduce the amount due from the customer on the new invoice.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Custom Email for Invoicing

When sending invoices directly from Rillet, you may want those invoices to come from your company's email domain instead of Rillet's so your customers recognize the email address.

To set up your custom email domain, navigate to Organization Settings > Invoices > Communications.

Step 1: Input your custom email

Then navigate to the Custom email section, click Edit.

Input the email which you would like your invoices to be sent from and press Save.

Step 2: Rillet verification

Once saved, Rillet will verify the email set up. You will see Pending Verification status while your email is being set up. Verification typically takes 24-48 hours.

Step 3: Update DNS settings

Once verification is complete. You will receive a file from Rillet which which contains CNAMEs. Your engineering team will need to add the CNAMEs specified to their DNS settings. Optionally, you can also add amazonses.com to your SPF record.

Once you have completed this step, you're all set to start sending invoices with your custom email!

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Turn Off Automatic Reminders

Automatic reminders in Rillet help ensure that customers receive timely notifications about their outstanding invoices. However, in some cases, you may want to disable these reminders—either for a specific customer, for all customers, or as the default setting for new customers.

Turn Off Automatic Reminders for a Specific Customer

Follow the steps below to turn off automatic reminders for a specific customer:

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Customers.

  1. Select the customer for whom you want to turn off automatic reminders.

  1. In the Settings section, locate the Automatic Reminders toggle and switch it off.

This change will only apply to the selected customer.

Turn Off Automatic Reminders for All Customers

If you want to disable automatic reminders globally, this action cannot be done through the interface. Please contact the Rillet team directly to request a global override.

Set Default Reminder Behavior for New Customers

To ensure that newly created customers do not have automatic reminders enabled by default:

  1. Go to Organization Settings > Invoices > Communications.
  2. Locate the Reminders option and ensure the toggle switch is turned off.
  3. Click Save to apply changes.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Sending Invoices

Not only can you create invoices in Rillet, you can send them to customers from within the platform as well. All of your invoices that need to be sent will be collected under the Workflow Snapshot section as Invoices to be sent.

These invoices all have a due date of today or earlier and have not been sent yet. Follow the steps below to send an invoice through Rillet.

Sending an Invoice Through Rillet

Click on Invoices to be sent. You’ll see a full list of all the invoices that need to be sent.

Find the invoice you’d like to send and click on the three dots in the far right column next to it. From the menu that appears, click Send.

A pop-up modal will appear, prompting you to review (or add) the email addresses you’d like to send the invoice to

  • Customer Emails - these are your customer email contacts
  • Organization Emails - this is your internal company email

Once you’ve confirmed all the recipient details, you can either elect to send yourself a preview of the invoice or send the invoice directly to the recipient by clicking either the Yes, confirm button or the Send me a preview button in the bottom right corner.

Once you send your invoice, its status will be changed from Unbilled to Unpaid, until the customer makes the payment.

Unless an invoice has a contact (email) added, you won’t be able to send it through Rillet and will be notified of such. To add a contact email, navigate to the customer's profile overview.

Marking an Invoice as Sent

In some cases, you may want or need to send an invoice to a customer through other means outside of Rillet. If that’s the case, you can mark that invoice as sent manually.

Follow the same steps above to navigate to your chosen unsent invoice and click on the three dots. Then, click Mark as Sent.

Just as in the sending invoice workflow, this will change your invoice's status from Unbilled to Unpaid, until your customer makes their payment.

Invoices that have been manually marked as sent will not be eligible for autopayment, and invoice reminders won't be sent for those invoices.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Add a Credit Memo

A credit memo is a document issued by a seller to reduce the amount a buyer owes after a mistake or change in a previous transaction. It can be used for many items, including:

  • to reduce the amount owed on an invoice due to a retrospective discount
  • a contract change
  • complete a cancellation
  • Reversal of an invoice
  • Write off for bad debt
  • Create a refund for a customer

In Rillet, Similar to contracts and invoices, credit memos can have long term revenue schedules and impact ARR based on the products selected.To add a Credit Memo:

To add a Credit Memo:

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Credit Memos.

  1. Select Add Credit Memo.

  1. Select a Customer.
  2. (Optional) Select an Invoice according to your needs:
    • If an invoice is selected, the credit memo will reduce the balance of that invoice
    • If no invoice is selected, the credit memo will remain open until it is either refunded to applied to another invoice.
  3. Select the Credit Memo date.
  4. Select a product in Add Product. Then, fill in the product information accordingly:
    • Description: Enter a description of your product.
    • Amount: Enter the amount this product costs per unit.
    • Quantity: Enter the Quantity.
    • Revenue Account: Update the GL Account if needed.
    • Revenue Recognition: Choose the Revenue Recognition dates. This will impact both the accounting revenue and the ARR metrics.
    • Tax:
      • Toggle right if you want to add tax information.
      • Enter a Tax Percentage and or choose the VAT rate.

  1. When you’re done filling in the information, select Save on the top right corner.

You will view your Credit Memo listed in the Accounts Receivable > Credit Memos page.

To credit an existing invoice

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Invoices.

  1. Select an invoice.
  2. Select the three-dot icon. Then, select Create credit memo.

This will automatically fill out the credit memo with all of the information from the invoice, but you can override any information needed.

FAQ

  • Will credit memos impact ARR?
    • If you use a product which is flagged to count towards ARR, then the credit memo will impact ARR negatively.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Create a New Product

You can create and manage products in Accounts Receivable to ensure accurate billing and revenue tracking. This guide helps you set up, update, and manage product details.

To Create a New Product:

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Products.

  1. Select + Add Product.

  1. Enter Product Details which are outlined below:
    1. Product Name (Internal): This is how you'd like to display this product internally.
    2. Invoice Line Item Description (optional): This is the description that will appear any time the corresponding customer is invoiced for your product. You can set this ahead of time so you don’t need to add it to each invoice individually, but you can always override this on each contract as needed.
    3. Revenue vs. Non Revenue Products: Whether it’s a SaaS tool or a professional service, it is likely that your product is considered a revenue product. If you aren’t returning a profit on the product, such as a billable expense, then it’s considered a non-revenue product.
    4. Revenue Type: In Rillet, there are two revenue types: Fixed and Usage. If you charge your customers a flat rate for a product, then it’s considered Fixed revenue. If you charge them a variable rate based on future consumption, then that product is considered Usage revenue.
    5. Revenue Account: This is the GL Account which will be used when revenue is recognized.
    6. Discount Account (optional): Optionally, you can choose to record discount contra revenue in a separate account from Revenue. If this is blank, net revenue recognition will be recorded to the Revenue Account. If this is selected, gross revenue will be recorded to the Revenue Account and discount contract revenue will be recorded in the Discount Account.
    7. Revenue Pattern: This field allows you to designate how frequently you’ll be recognize revenue. There are two options:
      • Even Period, Prorated First & Last
      • Daily.
    8. Count to MRR/ARR: Toggle this option as needed.
    9. Type (for Non-Revenue Products): Choose the type (e.g., "Fixed").
    10. Account (for Non-Revenue Products): Select the appropriate revenue account for this product.
  2. Once the general details are completed, click the Next Step button.

  1. Set Pricing Information:
    1. Pricing Type: Choose between Recurring or One-time.
    2. Default Price (per Period) (optional): Enter the default price (e.g., $1.00).
    3. Billing Period: Select the billing period (e.g., Monthly).
  2. After filling in the pricing information, click the Create Product button.

Managing Products

Once a product is created, you can edit, delete, deactivate, or reactivate its record.

To Edit a Product:

  1. Click on the three dots (•••) located at the end of the product’s row.
  2. Select Edit Product from the dropdown menu.

  1. Modify the Product Details.
  2. Click the Save Product button.

To Delete Product:

  1. Click on the three dots (•••) located at the end of the product’s row.
  2. Select Delete Product from the dropdown menu.

  1. Click Delete to confirm or Cancel if you do not wish to proceed.

Products can only be deleted if they have not been used on a contract or invoice.

To Deactivate Product:

  1. Click on the three dots (•••) located at the end of the product’s row.
  2. Select Deactivate Product from the dropdown menu.

  1. Click Deactivate to confirm or Cancel if you do not wish to proceed.

Note:

    • When you deactivate a product, it doesn't stop invoices and contracts from syncing with it. It only prevents the product from being used in new transactions.
    • Deactivated products can be reactivated later unless they have been permanently deleted.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Add a New Customer to Rillet

To get started sending invoices to your customers through Rillet, you’ll first need to add them to the system. Adding a customer is one of the steps you must take before you can begin using contracts.

📄 Getting Started with Accounts Receivable

To Add a New Customer:

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Customers.

  1. Select + Add Customer.

  1. Complete the New Customer Form:
    1. Company Name: Enter the customer's company name. Then, click Add more details to reveal additional fields.

    1. Invoicing Name (optional): The invoicing name auto-fills with the company name, but you can modify it if needed.
    2. Address:
      1. Address Line 1 (optional): Enter the main address.
      2. Address Line 2 (optional): Enter additional details.
      3. Country: Select the customer's country.
      4. City (optional): Enter the city.
      5. State (optional): Enter the state.
      6. Zip Code (optional): Enter the zip code.

    1. Shipping Address:
      1. Address Line 1 (optional): Enter the main address.
      2. Address Line 2 (optional): Enter additional details.
      3. Country: Select the customer's country.
      4. City (optional): Enter the city.
      5. State (optional): Enter the state.
      6. Zip Code (optional): Enter the zip code.

    1. Email(s):
      1. Click + Add email address.
      2. Enter the customer’s email.
      3. To add more emails, click + Add another email address.
      4. In the Send as field, choose whether the email should be the Main Sender or a CC.
    2. Notes (optional): Add any relevant details or comments.

    1. Automatic Reminder Settings:
      1. Enable automatic reminders to send emails to the customer.
      2. Click Set up reminders to configure the frequency and content of reminders.
    2. Online Payments:
      1. Enable this option to allow the customer to pay invoices online via Stripe.
      2. Click Set up Stripe to configure payment processing.
    3. Payment Terms: Select the desired payment terms (e.g., "Due on Receipt").
    4. Tax Rate: Set the tax rate (e.g., 0% or "Sales Tax").

    1. Department (optional): Assign a department if necessary.
    2. Customer Segment (optional): Specify a customer segment if needed.

  1. Click Create Customer to complete the process.

You’re all set! Now you’ll see your new customer listed along with your existing customers on this screen. If you’d like to start using contracts, you can now proceed to the next section, Products.

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments
  3. Add a New Customer to Rillet

Managing Customers

Once a customer is created, you can edit, delete, merge, or export customer records.

To Edit a Customer:

  1. Click on the three dots (•••) next to the customer’s name.
  2. Select Edit Customer from the dropdown menu.

  1. Modify the Customer Details.
  2. Click the Save Changes button.

  1. Click OK to confirm or Cancel if you do not want to proceed with the changes.

To Delete Customer:

  1. Click on the three dots (•••) located at the end of the customer’s row.
  2. Select Delete Customer from the dropdown menu.

Alternative Method:

    1. Click on the three dots (•••) next to the customer’s name.
    2. Select Edit Customer from the dropdown menu.
    3. In the Edit Customer view, you will find the Delete Customer button located at the end of the customer details section.
  1. Click Delete to confirm or Cancel if you do not wish to proceed.

To Merge Customers:

  1. Select the customers to merge.

Note:

You can only select 2 customers to merge at a time. Ensure you have selected the correct customers before proceeding with the merge.

  1. Click the Merge Customers button at the bottom of the screen.

  1. A pop-up window titled "Choose main customer" will appear. Select the main customer you want to keep from the options presented. This will retain their name, details, and avatar.
  2. After selecting the main customer, click the Proceed with merge button to finalize the action.

To Export Customers:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the + Add Customer button.
  2. Select Export customers.

The customer data will be exported automatically in XLSX format.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments
  3. Add a New Customer to Rillet

Invoice a New Customer

To manage your invoices efficiently in Rillet, you have two options: creating invoices directly for a new customer or generating them through Contracts. While both methods work, it's recommended to use Contracts as they integrate the invoice with the terms of the agreement, ensuring smoother billing and revenue recognition.

Option 1: Create an Invoice Directly for a New Customer

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Customers.

  1. Choose the customer you just created.

  1. Select Invoices.

  1. Click + Add Invoice.

  1. Enter the following invoice information:
    1. General Information
      1. Customer: Select the customer for whom the invoice is created.
      2. Invoice Date: Enter the date the invoice is issued.
      3. Due Date: Specify when the payment is due.
      4. Terms: Choose the payment terms.
      5. Purchase Order Number (optional): Enter the PO number if provided by the customer.
      6. Memo (optional): Add additional notes or information related to the invoice.

    1. Product Details
      1. Description: Provide a clear description of the product or service being invoiced.
      2. Price: Enter the product's unit price.
      3. Quantity: Specify the units sold.
      4. Subtotal: This is automatically calculated based on the price and quantity.
      5. Tax: Toggle on or off based on whether the tax applies.
        • Tax Percentage: Enter the applicable tax rate (e.g., "0%").
        • Tax Type: Specify the type of tax being applied (e.g., "Sales Tax").
      6. Revenue Account: Select the product's associated revenue account.
      7. Revenue Recognition: Choose the revenue recognition date.
      8. Discount: Toggle on or off based on whether a discount applies. If enabled, specify the discount amount or percentage.
      9. Customer Segment (optional): If applicable, indicate the segment or category of the customer.
      10. Tag (optional): Add relevant tags to help organize or track the invoice.

  1. Click Save.

Option 2: Create an Invoice through a Contract

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Contracts.

  1. Select + Add Contract.

  1. Complete the necessary information, such as the contract name and duration, ensuring the customer and products are set up.

Note:

If you need detailed instructions on how to add a contract, refer to the Add a Contract article.

This method allows you to link the invoice directly to the terms of the agreement, which can simplify future invoicing and improve the accuracy of revenue recognition.

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Write Off Bad Debt

Writing off bad debt allows organizations to remove uncollectible accounts from their books, improving the accuracy of financial reporting.

To write off bad debt:

  1. Create a new Product named Bad Debt.
  2. Set the product as a Non-Revenue Product.

  1. Click Create Product.

  1. Locate the invoice that needs to be written off.
  2. Click the three points (...) at the end of the invoice row.
  3. Select Create Credit Memo from the dropdown list.

  1. In the credit memo, change the product to Bad Debt.
  2. Click Save to finalize the credit memo.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Add billing periods to invoices

A billing period defines the timeframe for which charges apply to an invoice. It helps indicate when a service was provided or a product was billed, ensuring accuracy in financial records.

To adding billing periods to invoices from Contracts:

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Contracts.

  1. Locate the contract you want to edit and click three dots (...) on the final row.
  2. Select Edit Contract from the dropdown menu.

  1. Go to the Invoicing tab.
  2. Select the Edit icon.

  1. Modify the start and end dates for the billing period as needed.

Note:

    • If an invoice is generated from a contract, its billing period must be edited in the contract.
    • A one-day billing period will not be displayed on the invoice PDF.
  1. Click the Confirm contract to save changes.

To Add Billing Periods to Standalone Invoices:

  1. Select + Add Invoice or open an existing invoice.
  2. In the invoice settings, locate the section to set revenue recognition dates.
  3. Define the start and end dates for the billing period.
  4. After setting the billing period, save the invoice to apply changes.

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Invoicing & Payments

Create a New Invoice

Rillet simplifies the invoicing process, providing tools to create, send, and manage invoices efficiently. Whether you’re using email or online payments, Rillet keeps your financial operations smooth.

Invoicing Fees:

  • Rillet doesn’t charge fees if you send invoices by email and the customer pays using the ACH bank details on the invoice.
  • If you use online payments or autopay through Rillet with Stripe, you'll be charged the standard Stripe fees.

To create a new invoice:

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Invoices.

  1. Select + Add Invoice.

  1. Enter the following invoice information:
    1. General Information
      1. Select Customer: Select the customer from the drown-down list to create the invoice.
      2. Rillet invoicing Account: Select the invoicing account.
      3. Invoice Date: Specify the date the invoice is issued (e.g., "01/17/2025").
      4. Due Date: Set the payment deadline (e.g., "01/17/2025").
      5. Terms: Choose the payment terms (e.g., "Due on Receipt").
      6. Purchase Order Number (optional): Enter the PO number if provided by the customer.
      7. Memo (optional): Add any additional notes or information related to the invoice.

    1. Product Details
      1. Description: Provide a clear description of the product or service being invoiced (e.g., "Sample product").
      2. Price: Enter the product's unit price (e.g., "$5304.96").
      3. Quantity: Specify the units sold (e.g., "1").
      4. Subtotal: This is automatically calculated based on the price and quantity (e.g., "$5304.96").
      5. Tax: Toggle on or off based on whether the tax applies.
        • Tax Percentage: Enter the applicable tax rate (e.g., "0%").
        • Tax Type: Specify the type of tax being applied (e.g., "Sales Tax").
      6. Revenue Account: Select the product's associated revenue account (e.g., "Product").
      7. Revenue Recognition: Choose the revenue recognition date (e.g., "01/17/2025").
      8. Discount: Toggle on or off based on whether a discount applies. If enabled, specify the discount amount or percentage.
      9. Customer Segment (optional): If applicable, indicate the segment or category of the customer.
      10. Tag (optional): Add relevant tags to help organize or track the invoice.

  1. Click Save.

Managing Invoices

Once an invoice is created, you can edit, delete, deactivate, or reactivate its record.

To Send an Invoice:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the invoice you want to send.
  2. Select Send from the dropdown menu.

  1. In the Send Invoice window:
    1. Customer Email: Make sure the email address is correct. It will be auto-filled based on the customer's setup.
    2. Send As: This field displays the preconfigured value for the customer and cannot be modified.
    3. Add Another Address (Optional): If needed, click Add another address and provide the additional email. Otherwise, skip this step.
  2. Choose one of the following options:
    1. Click Yes, confirm to send the invoice immediately.
    2. Click Send me a preview to review the invoice before sending.

To Mark an Invoice as Sent:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the invoice you want to mark as sent.
  2. Select Mark as sent from the dropdown menu.

Once selected, the invoice status will change from "Unbilled" to "Unpaid", ensuring it is accurately tracked in your records.

To Receive Payment for an Invoice:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the invoice you want to mark as paid.
  2. Select Receive Payment from the dropdown menu.

  1. In the Receive Payment window, fill in the following fields:
    1. Amount: Ensure the amount is correct (e.g., $5,304.96).
    2. Date: Select the payment date.
    3. Account: Choose the account where the payment will be recorded (e.g., "Mercury Checking").
  2. Click Confirm to finish the payment.

Once completed, the invoice status will update from "Unbilled" to "Paid (Uncleared)".

To Download an Invoice:

  1. Click the three dots (•••) next to the invoice you want to download.
  2. Select Download from the dropdown menu.

The invoice will automatically be downloaded in PDF format.

Additional Information:

  • Invoice Updates: Changes to addresses, tax IDs, or payment instructions will apply to all invoices except those in closed periods, credited, partially credited, or paid statuses.
  • Service Periods on Invoices: Automatically displayed for billing schedules (Monthly, Quarterly, Yearly) but not for customized schedules.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable

Usage Based & Minimum Commitments

Create a Usage-Based ProductAdd a Usage-Based ContractUpload Usage DataSet Up the Usage Minimum CommitmentSet Up Commitment ContractsRemove Usage AmountsUpdate Existing Usage Amounts

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Usage Based & Minimum Commitments

Create a Usage-Based Product

Creating a usage-based product enables businesses to implement flexible pricing based on actual consumption. This process helps configure products that charge customers according to their usage, ensuring billing is both accurate and aligned with customer activity.

Requirements

See 📄 Create a New Product for basic guide on creating products.

Create a Usage-Based Product

To create a usage-based product:

  1. Select the following
    1. Product Type = Revenue Product
    2. Revenue Type = Usage
  2. Click Next Step.

  1. In the Pricing structure dropdown menu, select one of the following options:
    1. Standard
    2. Graduated (tiered)
      1. This defines a price per unit within a range.
      2. Assuming you tiers are: Units 1-10 will be charged at $10, and Units 11+ will be charged at $5, If a customer has 11 units, the total cost will be $105 ($10 X 10 units + $5 x 1 unit)
    3. Volume (tiered)
      1. This defines one price for all units.
      2. Assuming you tiers are: Units 1-10 $10, and Units 11+ $5, If a customer has 11 units, the total cost will be $55 ($5 x 11 units)
  2. Billing Period defaults to monthly.
  3. Define the rates.
    1. From: The minimum usage for that tier.
    2. Up To: The maximum usage for that tier.
    3. Unit Price: The cost per unit for that tier.
    4. Flat Price: Any additional flat fee applicable for the tier.
  4. Click Save Product to complete the setup.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Usage Based & Minimum Commitments

Add a Usage-Based Contract

Adding a usage-based contract establishes clear terms for billing customers based on consumption. This process involves defining contract details, selecting products, and confirming all relevant information to ensure accurate and consistent invoicing.

Before you start

See the 📄 Adding Contracts to Rillet guide.

Add a Usage-Based Contract

To add a usage-based contract:

  1. Fill in the contract details.
  2. Click Next Step.

  1. Select + Add product.

  1. Select the usage-based product that you want to use.

  1. Verify that all products are correctly listed with their details and make changes as needed to deviate from the standard pricing.

  1. Set up the desired invoicing date(s) and payment terms.

  1. Check the overall summary to confirm that all information is accurate and consistent.
  2. Click Confirm Contract to finalize.

It is expected that all metrics and contract values will be zero upon initial creation unless you have commitments also on the contract.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Usage Based & Minimum Commitments

Upload Usage Data

Loading usage data allows businesses to track customer consumption and generate accurate invoices. By importing CSV files to Rillet, businesses can update usage information and ensure that invoices reflect the actual usage, maintaining accuracy in the billing process.

To add usage data:

  1. Navigate to the usage contract and click the three dots (or the options icon) next to the contract.
  2. Select Upload Usage from the dropdown menu. The Upload Usage window will open.

  1. Click Download Template to get the CSV template.

  1. Fill out the template with the corresponding usage data.

Note: The downloaded template will pre-populate with active usage contract data.

  1. Return to the Upload Usage window and select Import via CSV.

  1. Choose the completed CSV file and click Upload.

  1. In the Contracts tab, you can verify essential information such as the contract type (e.g., Usage Contract), dates, and total value.

  1. After uploading usage, it is important to review the generated invoices. Here, you can check the totals and specific details related to the flat fee and unit pricing.

This process ensures that customers know what they need to pay and allows them to review and confirm that all data is accurate before making a payment.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Usage Based & Minimum Commitments

Set Up the Usage Minimum Commitment

The Usage Minimum Commitment feature allows you to establish a baseline spending level that is recorded separately from your normal revenue. By configuring this setting, you ensure that the minimum usage spend is tracked accurately, with invoices generated at the chosen frequency.

To set up the usage minimum commitment:

  1. Go to Organization Settings > Accounting.

  1. In the Usage Minimum Commitment section, you need to set the following:
    • Revenue Account: Choose the revenue GL account where the minimum commitment spend will be recorded. You are advised to use a separate GL account from your regular revenue.
    • Default Invoice Frequency: Select the invoicing frequency that will apply to the minimum commitment (e.g., monthly, yearly).
  2. Click Save Changes to apply your settings.

After these configurations, you can Set Up Commitment Contracts.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Usage Based & Minimum Commitments

Set Up Commitment Contracts

Once you have configured the Usage Minimum Commitment, you can set up commitment contracts (both monthly and contract-based) to help users understand their spending commitments.

To set up commitment contracts:

  1. Go to Accounts Receivable > Customers.

  1. Select a Customer for whom you want to add the contract.

  1. Click the Contract tab.
  2. Select the + Add Contract button.

  1. Enter Contract Details:
    1. Customer: This will be automatically filled and cannot be modified.
    2. Contract Name: Enter a descriptive name for the contract (e.g., "Demo Contract").
    3. Contract Type: Choose one from the following:
      • New Sales
      • Existing
      • Expansion
      • Reactivation
      • Contraction
    4. Start Date: Select the start date for the contract.
    5. Close Date: Select the date of finalizing the deal.
    6. Duration: Choose from the following options:
      • 6 months
      • 1 year
      • 2 years
      • 3 years
      • Open-ended
      • Custom
    7. End Date (Optional): Select the end date for the contract.
  2. Click Next Step.

  1. Click + Add Product.

  1. Select a product with the revenue type set as Usage.

  1. Set Commitment Details:
    1. Usage Cycle: The usage cycle defines how often usage is invoiced. Choose between monthly or contract.

Note:

      • If monthly, the commitment will be split (e.g., $1,000 per month for a $12,000 total).
      • If contract, the total can be consumed at any time within the contract duration.
    1. Minimum Contract Commitment: Minimum applies across all Usage-based products. Enter the total commitment amount for the contract.
  1. Click Next Step.

  1. In the Invoicing section, fill in the following fields:
    1. Invoicing Frequency: Select Monthly, Quarterly, or Yearly for invoicing frequency.
    2. Month and day of invoice: Select the day of the month for invoicing (e.g., 1st, 31st).
    3. Payment Terms: Specify payment terms.
      • Due on Receipt
      • Net 10
      • Net 30
      • Net 60
      • Custom
  2. Click Next Step.

  1. Confirm Revenue Schedule and Click Next Step.
    1. Usage Cycle Contract example:

    1. Usage Cycle Monthly example:

  1. Click Confirm Contract

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Usage Based & Minimum Commitments

Remove Usage Amounts

If a usage entry was incorrect or needs to be removed, you can delete it by setting the amount to zero.

To remove usage amounts:

  1. Download the Usage Upload template. To delete the usage amount, follow the steps below within the template:
    1. Duplicate the Previous Line.
    2. Change the date for the new entry (Use the same date as in the Update Existing Usage Amounts methods).
    3. Set the usage amount to zero (0) for both dates.

  1. Save the file.
  2. Click the three dots (...) menu and select Upload Usage.

  1. Click Import via CSV to upload the template.

  1. Drag and Drop the file or click Select File to upload it manually.

  1. Click Upload to confirm.

  1. Return to the Customer Contracts page.

  1. Go to the Invoices tab.

Check that there are no invoices associated with the removed usage amount.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Receivable
  2. Usage Based & Minimum Commitments

Update Existing Usage Amounts

Once you've uploaded usage, you might need to adjust an entry - either by replacing an existing amount or adding more usage on a different date.

Method 1: Override Existing Usage

To update usage amount using the override:

  1. Download the "Upload Usage" template and update the usage amount for the same date as previously uploaded.
    1. Note: Validate that columns A-F also match identically to the previous upload.

  1. Save the file.
  2. Click the three dots (...) menu and select Upload Usage.

  1. Click Import via CSV to upload the template.

  1. Drag and Drop the file or click Select File to upload it manually.

  1. Click Upload to confirm the changes.

  1. Return to the Customer Contracts page.

  1. Go to the Invoices tab.
  2. Select the Invoice Number.

  1. Check that the updated usage is reflected correctly.

You will see the usage changed, overriding the previous amount.

Method 2: Add to Existing Usage

To update a usage by adding it to an existing usage:

  1. Download the "Upload Usage" template and update the usage amount for the different date than previously uploaded.
  2. Update the file with the number of units of usage you would like to add to the previous value

  1. Save the file.
  2. Click the three dots (...) menu and select Upload Usage.

  1. Click Import via CSV to upload the template.

  1. Click Upload to confirm.

  1. Return to the Customer Contracts page.

  1. Go to the Invoices tab.
  2. Select the Invoice Number.

  1. Check that the total usage is reflected correctly.

The invoice will now reflect the updated total usage, combining the previously recorded amount with the newly added entry.

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Payable
  2. Vendors

Add a New Vendor

Rillet streamlines vendor management by providing an intuitive platform where you can effortlessly add, organize, and manage vendor information. With built-in features for tracking vendor details, contact information, contracts, and payment terms, Rillet ensures that all your vendor data is easily accessible in one place.

To add a new vendor:

  1. Go to Accounts Payable > Vendors.

  1. Select + Add Vendors.

  1. Enter the following vendor information:
    1. General Information
      1. Vendor Name: Enter the vendor's name (e.g., "Demo Vendor").
      2. Point Person (Optional): Specify a contact person for this vendor.
      3. Email (optional): Enter the vendor's email address for communication.
      4. Address Line 1 (optional): Input the primary address for the vendor.
      5. Address Line 2 (optional): Provide additional address information if necessary.
      6. Country (Optional): Select the vendor's country from a dropdown list.
      7. City (optional): Enter the city where the vendor is located.
      8. State (optional): Specify the vendor's state.
      9. Zip Code (optional): Enter the zip code of the vendor's location.
      10. Tax ID (optional): Provide the vendor's tax identification number.
      11. 1099-Eligible (Optional): Check this box if the vendor is 1099-eligible.

    1. Payment Information
      1. Payment Method (Optional): Select the payment methods from available options:
        • Bank
        • Credit card
        • Debit Card
      2. Payment Terms (Optional): Select the payment terms from available options:
        • Due on Receipt
        • Net 10
        • Net 30
        • Net 60

    1. Default Coding
      1. Default Account (Optional): Choose the default account for this vendor.
      2. Department (Optional): Specify the department associated with this vendor.
      3. Customer Segment (Optional): Select the customer segment related to the vendor.

Note: These default settings will be used automatically when creating new quick entries and will be applied in Rillet’s automatic entry workflows.

  1. Click Save.

Once you click Save, you will see a message confirming that the vendor has been successfully saved.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Payable

Expense Transactions

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Payable

Prepaid Expenses

Making Prepaid Schedule AdjustmentsHow to create a Prepaid ExpensePrepaid Expenses

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Payable
  2. Prepaid Expenses

Making Prepaid Schedule Adjustments

We’re excited to announce a new Rillet feature: prepaid adjustments. This feature allows you to quickly add prepayments to journal entries and can help you keep better track of payments made by your customers. To set up prepaid adjustments, read the steps below.

First, go to the Bills page by clicking on the receipt icon in your left navigation bar. Then, click Bills.

Next, navigate to one of the bills you have already created. Make sure the bill you select has a prepaid schedule service period.

To edit the prepaid schedule, click on the three dots in the upper right corner. Then, select Adjust Prepaid Schedule from the dropdown menu.

You’ll now see the prepaid schedule per line item on your bill. Line items within a current schedule will be grayed out if they are in a closed period. If you need to edit these line items, you’ll need to unlock the time periods they fall under.

You can edit any of the line items that are not grayed out by filling out your desired information in the date and/or amount fields.

You can also delete line items if you so choose by clicking on the trash can icon.

To save your changes, click the Confirm button in the bottom right corner.

To see how your updates impact the corresponding GL account, click on the three dots again and then click GL Impact from the dropdown menu.

Here, you’ll see that the journal entries are updated your schedule adjustments.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Payable
  2. Prepaid Expenses

How to create a Prepaid Expense

When creating a new Bill, you may notice that the Prepaid Expenses accounts are greyed out and cannot be selected. This is intended to allow you to create an more automated prepaid expense schedule

How to properly create a Prepaid Expense entry

  1. Open the AP/Expense creation form by going to Add Bill.
  2. Locate the GL account field and select the appropriate Expense GL account which you would like the amortization to be recorded to. Do not select the greyed-out "Prepaid Expenses" option.
  3. Set a service period on the Bill.

That's it!

Why this works

Each Expense account in your Chart of Accounts is already linked to the corresponding Prepaid Account. When you select the appropriate Expense GL account, Rillet now automatically associates it with the correct Prepaid Account. This ensures that the entry will be reflected correctly in the Prepaid Schedule.

Key points to remember

  • Always select the appropriate Expense GL account when creating a Prepaid Expense entry.
  • The system will automatically handle the Prepaid Account association based on your Expense GL selection.
  • To review your amortization schedule review the GL Impact on the Bill or navigate to the Prepaid Schedule.

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Payable
  2. Prepaid Expenses

Prepaid Expenses

Prepaid expenses are costs paid in advance for future services, like insurance, rent, or subscriptions. They are recorded as assets and gradually spread out over time to match the service period, ensuring costs are properly assigned to the right accounting periods.

How Does the Prepaid Schedule Work?

The platform automatically includes any bill with a service period longer than one day in the prepaid schedule. Additionally:

  • Monthly straight-line amortization is calculated based on the service period of the bill.
  • Amortization entries are automatically posted in the system.

Amortization Methods

You can choose between two options for amortization calculations:

  1. Click the company icon in the top right corner of the screen.
  2. Go to Organization Settings > Accounting.

  1. Locate the Straight-line Amortization section:
    1. Prepaid Expenses
      1. Daily: Calculates amortization based on the exact number of days in each reporting month.
      2. Even Period: Distributes amounts equally across full months, with daily calculations applied only in the first and last months.

Viewing and Adjusting the Prepaid Schedule

Prepaid Schedule

All accounts classified as Prepaid Expenses are grouped in the schedule.

  1. Go to Reporting > Close Reports.
  2. Choose the type of Prepaid Schedule.

  1. Set the Selected Period to view the monthly amortization breakdown for a specific time frame.

  1. Use the Prepaid Balance as of field to see the prepaid balance up to a specific month.

Note:

Transactions entered as journal entries appear in the prepaid balance on the balance sheet but not in the prepaid schedule.

How to Set Up Prepaid Expense Accounts

Note:

  • Prepaid amounts will automatically be allocated to the General Ledger (GL) based on the expense account selected on the bill.
  • This setup also works for other transaction types, including expenses such as bills, charges, and reimbursements.

To Create a Prepaid Expense Account:

  1. Go to the Chart of Accounts menu.
  2. Create as many prepaid expense accounts as needed.
  3. Assign each expense account to a corresponding prepaid account in the chart of accounts settings.

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Accounts Payable

Charges

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Close Management

Close Checklist

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Close Management
  2. Close Checklist

Intro to the Close Checklist

A well-structured accounting close checklist ensures an efficient and accurate month-end close process. This guide will walk you through the basics of Rillet's Close Checklist, including adding tasks, assigning owners, setting due dates, and attaching relevant files.

Create a New Task

  • Click on the “Add Task” button.
  • Enter a task name that clearly describes the accounting activity (e.g., “Reconcile Bank Statements”).
  • Click Save to add the task to your checklist.

Assign an Owner

  1. Select the task from the checklist and click "Edit"
  2. In the Owner column, select the user you would like to assign the task to. Any Rillet user can be assigned a task.
  3. Click "Save".

Set Due Dates

  1. Select the task from the checklist and click "Edit"
  2. In the Due Date column, input the date the task should be completed by.
  3. Click "Save".

Attach & View Supporting Files

  1. From the task list, click on the Paper Clip icon.

  1. Drag and Drop or select the relevant file that you would like to attach.
